rows.requirement: Pagination response MUST include has_next_page
rows.quote: "required": ["has_next_page"]
rows.notes: response $defs requires has_next_page

rows.requirement: When has_next_page is true, cursor MUST be present (if/then schema constraint)
rows.notes: Schema-enforced via if/then; prose at L30 says cursor MUST be present when has_next_page is true

rows.requirement: Cursor to fetch next page MUST be present when has_next_page is true
rows.quote: "description": "Cursor to fetch the next page of results. MUST be present when has_next_page is true."
rows.notes: Prose-in-schema restatement of the if/then constraint

rows.requirement: Cursors are opaque strings (implementations MAY encode as stateless keyset tokens)
rows.quote:
Cursors are opaque strings
that implementations MAY encode as stateless keyset tokens.
rows.notes: Opacity is a contract on clients; schema types cursor as string only

rows.requirement: limit request parameter has minimum 1 and default 10
rows.notes: minimum:1 and default:10 schema-enforced; clamp is MAY

rows.requirement: Implementations SHOULD accept a page size of at least 10
rows.quote: Implementations SHOULD accept a page size of at least 10.

rows.requirement: limit is a hint; implementations MAY clamp to their maximum silently, returning fewer results without error
rows.quote:
The `limit` parameter is a requested page size, not a guaranteed count.
Implementations SHOULD accept a page size of at least 10. When the
requested limit exceeds the implementation's maximum, implementations
MAY clamp to their maximum silently — returning fewer results without
error.

rows.requirement: Clients MUST NOT assume the response size equals the requested limit
rows.quote: Clients MUST NOT assume the response size equals the requested limit.
rows.notes: Client-side constraint; not server-testable

rows.requirement: A valid search request MUST include at least one of: a query string, one or more filters, or an extension-defined input
rows.quote:
A valid search request MUST include at least one of: a `query` string,
one or more `filters`, or an extension-defined input.
rows.notes: search_request schema has no anyOf/required enforcing this; prose-only

rows.requirement: Implementations MUST validate that incoming requests contain at least one recognized input
rows.quote:
Implementations MUST validate that incoming requests contain at least one
recognized input

rows.requirement: Implementations SHOULD reject empty or invalid requests with an appropriate error
rows.quote:
SHOULD reject empty or invalid requests with an
appropriate error.

rows.requirement: Empty search returns products:[] with no messages (not an error)
rows.quote: When search finds no matches, return an empty array without messages.
rows.notes: L200: "This is not an error - the query was valid but returned no results." Phrased as instruction, not RFC keyword

rows.requirement: Lookup MUST support lookup by product ID and variant ID
rows.quote:
Implementations MUST support
lookup by product ID and variant ID.
rows.notes: Also restated in catalog_lookup.json#L35 ids description

rows.requirement: get_product MUST support product ID and variant ID for the single id parameter
rows.quote: "description": "Product or variant identifier. Implementations MUST support product ID and variant ID."
rows.notes: Prose-in-schema; not structurally enforced

rows.requirement: Duplicate identifiers in the request MUST be deduplicated
rows.quote: Duplicate identifiers in the request MUST be deduplicated.
rows.notes: Prose only; schema does not enforce uniqueItems on ids

rows.requirement: When multiple identifiers resolve to the same product, it MUST be returned once
rows.quote:
When multiple identifiers
resolve to the same product, it MUST be returned once.
rows.notes: Prose only

rows.requirement: Variants without an inputs entry MUST NOT appear in lookup responses
rows.quote:
Variants without an `inputs` entry MUST NOT appear in
lookup responses.
rows.notes: Partly schema-enforced: lookup_variant requires inputs with minItems:1 (catalog_lookup.json#L13-L20), so any variant present must carry inputs; presence/exclusion logic itself is prose

rows.requirement: lookup response variants MUST include a non-empty inputs array (correlation metadata)
rows.notes: lookup_variant allOf adds required inputs minItems:1

rows.requirement: For get_product / catalog/product, an unknown identifier (NOT_FOUND) returns HTTP 200 with ucp.status:"error" and a descriptive message
rows.quote:
When the identifier does not resolve to a product, the server returns HTTP 200
with `ucp.status: "error"` and a descriptive message. This is an application
outcome, not a transport error — the handler executed and reports its result
via the UCP envelope.
rows.notes: Stated descriptively; MUST keyword inferred from "returns HTTP 200". Conformance L575 reinforces HTTP 200 for lookup

rows.requirement: All application-level outcomes (incl. not-found) return HTTP 200 with the UCP envelope and optional messages array
rows.quote:
All application-level outcomes return HTTP 200 with the UCP envelope and optional
`messages` array.

rows.requirement: When all batch identifiers fail lookup, products array is empty; response MAY include informational not_found messages
rows.quote:
When all requested identifiers fail lookup, the `products` array is empty. The response
MAY include informational messages indicating which identifiers were not found.
rows.notes: Batch lookup (HTTP 200) differs from get_product single-resource error

rows.requirement: When the Lookup capability is advertised, both /catalog/lookup and /catalog/product MUST be available
rows.quote: When the Lookup capability is advertised, both `/catalog/lookup` and `/catalog/product` MUST be available.

rows.requirement: REST transport MUST implement endpoints for each advertised catalog capability per its requirements
rows.quote:
A conforming REST transport implementation **MUST**:

1. Implement endpoints for each catalog capability advertised in the business's UCP profile, per their respective capability requirements ([Search](search.md), [Lookup](lookup.md)).

rows.requirement: REST transport MUST support cursor-based pagination with default limit of 10
rows.quote: 3. Support cursor-based pagination with default limit of 10.
rows.notes: Conformance item; default 10 also in pagination.json#L19

rows.requirement: REST transport MUST return HTTP 200 for lookup requests; unknown identifiers result in fewer products returned
rows.quote: 4. Return HTTP 200 for lookup requests; unknown identifiers result in fewer products returned (MAY include informational `not_found` messages).

rows.requirement: REST transport MUST return HTTP 400 with request_too_large error for requests exceeding batch size limits
rows.quote: 5. Return HTTP 400 with `request_too_large` error for requests exceeding batch size limits.
rows.notes: Lookup.md#L77 restates MUST reject; HTTP 400 request_too_large for REST

rows.requirement: Lookup implementations MAY enforce a maximum batch size and MUST reject requests exceeding their limit with an appropriate error
rows.quote:
Implementations
MAY enforce a maximum batch size and MUST reject requests exceeding their limit
with an appropriate error (HTTP 400 `request_too_large` for REST, JSON-RPC
`-32602` for MCP).

rows.requirement: Lookup ids array is required with minItems 1
search_response MUST include ucp and products
rows.quote:
"required": [
        "ucp",
        "products"
      ]
rows.notes: Empty-results case is products:[] (still required, present as empty array)

rows.requirement: lookup_response MUST include ucp and products
rows.quote:
"required": [
        "ucp",
        "products"
      ]

rows.requirement: get_product_response MUST include ucp and a singular product object
rows.quote:
"required": [
        "ucp",
        "product"
      ]

rows.requirement: For Variant ID lookups via get_product, the requested variant MUST be the first element (featured)
rows.quote: **Variant ID**: The requested variant MUST be the first element (featured).

rows.requirement: get_product response MUST include product.selected when the product has configurable options
rows.quote:
The response MUST include `product.selected` when the product has
configurable options
rows.notes: Conditional requirement; schema lists selected but not unconditionally required

rows.requirement: In get_product selected input, each option name MUST appear at most once
rows.quote: Each option name MUST appear at most once.
rows.notes: Reclassified 2026-07-02: the MUST binds the CLIENT'S request `selected` input (lookup.md '#### Input'), not the merchant — same subject-binding as CAT-008. No merchant-observable obligation exists (the spec does not mandate a rejection of duplicate option names), so a merchant check would grade something the spec never requires of merchants. Response-side product.selected uniqueness is not normatively stated either. Candidate for a documented client-side exemption in Phase 4.

rows.requirement: Eligibility and policy enforcement MUST occur at checkout time using binding transaction data (catalog context signals are provisional)
rows.quote:
Eligibility and policy enforcement MUST occur at checkout time using
binding transaction data.
rows.notes: Cross-cuts catalog/checkout; relevant to catalog context handling

rows.requirement: Signal values MUST NOT be buyer-asserted claims
rows.quote: Signal values MUST NOT be buyer-asserted claims.

rows.requirement: Catalog responses SHOULD NOT be reused across sessions without re-validation
rows.quote:
Responses can be session-specific and **SHOULD NOT** be
reused across sessions without re-validation.

rows.requirement: Disclosure warnings (presentation:"disclosure") carry notices that platforms must not hide or dismiss
rows.quote:
Warnings with `presentation: "disclosure"` carry notices (e.g., allergen
declarations, safety warnings) that platforms must not hide or dismiss.
rows.notes: Lowercase "must not"; rendering contract is in checkout.md (out of scope here)

notes: Catalog (dev.ucp.shopping.catalog.search / .lookup) is NEW in version 2026-04-08 — absent in the 2026-01-23 release. All rows are tagged versions:["2026-04-08"] only.
NO official conformance-suite oracle exists for catalog; every row has official_oracle=false.

SECURITY-RELEVANT FINDING (requested): There is NO requirement in any of the in-scope files (catalog/index.md, search.md, lookup.md, rest.md; product.json, variant.json) that platforms MUST strip scripts / event-handlers / untrusted HTML from product or variant descriptions. product.json references a shared description.json (formats like plain/markdown), and index.md#L169-171 points to the checkout.md "Warning Presentation" rendering contract — neither of those files is in the allowed read set, so no such normative could be quoted. If a sanitize/strip-HTML MUST exists in UCP, it lives in description.json or checkout.md (out of scope), not in the catalog capability docs. No row was emitted for it.

Pagination if/then constraint (has_next_page const true => required cursor) is the only structurally schema-enforced cross-field rule (CAT-002, schema_enforced=true). Dedup (CAT-015/016), variant-exclusion logic (CAT-017 partial), search input-presence (CAT-009/010), and not-found-HTTP-200 (CAT-019/020) are PROSE requirements => schema_enforced=false, except where backed by a structural constraint (lookup_variant required inputs minItems:1 = CAT-017/CAT-018, ids required = CAT-028, response required arrays = CAT-029..031).

Several MUST/MUST-NOT statements (CAT-008 client-side, CAT-035/036/037 cross-cutting, CAT-038 lowercase "must not") are manual/untestable from a server conformance standpoint; marked testability=manual.
limit: schema enforces minimum:1 and default:10 (CAT-005). 'limit is a hint / MAY clamp / clients MUST NOT assume count==limit' (CAT-007/008) is prose in search.md, not enforceable by schema.

Empty-search behavior (CAT-012) is written as an instruction ("return an empty array without messages", "This is not an error") rather than with an RFC-2119 keyword; classified MUST by intent and flagged in notes.
